qa_pairs.question: How can one verify that the Virtual IP (VIP) is successfully taken over by the standby node?
qa_pairs.answer: To verify that the IP is taken over by the standby node, you simply need to stop the `keepalived` service on the active system.

qa_pairs.question: Which commands can be used to check if the Virtual IP (VIP) has been transferred to the standby system?
qa_pairs.answer: You can check if the Virtual IP (VIP) has been transferred by using either the `ip a s dev ens3` command or the `systemctl status` command.

qa_pairs.question: What is the command to stop the `keepalived` service on the active system, such as `web-srv-1`?
qa_pairs.answer: To stop the `keepalived` service on the active system, you would execute `systemctl stop keepalived`.

qa_pairs.question: What is observed when running `ip a s dev ens3` on the active system (web-srv-1) after stopping `keepalived`?
qa_pairs.answer: After stopping `keepalived` on the active system (web-srv-1), running `ip a s dev ens3` will show that the Virtual IP address, such as 192.168.27.251/32, is no longer associated with the `ens3` interface on that system.
